mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls transmits into higher aggregate income and consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When triggered, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal's rank correlation with forward returns is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate and a month-over-month decrease in consumer price inflation indicates rising ex-post real interest rates, which tighten financial conditions and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between month-over-month changes in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows a rank correlation with forward returns that is positive or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in industrial production signals an overheating goods-producing sector that prompts additional Federal Reserve restraint, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative over a 2-year rolling window within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in retail sales below its trailing 3-month average signals a contraction in consumer spending momentum that reduces expected corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when RSAFS falls below its 3-month average over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates applied to corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of months, or the rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls signal a coordinated contraction in income and spending, amplifying recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances, or the interaction term rank IC is above zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accommodative monetary policy, a month-over-month increase in housing starts signals successful transmission of low borrowing costs into real economic activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, month-over-month increases in HOUST are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal rank correlation is negative over a 2-year rolling window within this regime.
